scope: the unifying safe-regeneration contract across all generators
summary: Synthesizes the cross-cutting contract: single-target by default, reject unsupported roots, skip z_future and external, write only semantic deltas, specs-root-relative IDs, preserve manual and parent fields, closed enums, then hand changed metadata to scoped incremental indexing.

title: Add an explicit scoped regeneration boundary
problem: backfill has no spec-folder positional argument and ignores unknown positional args, walking from root. Unsupported paths only fail later in refreshGraphMetadataForSpecFolder. z_future is already excluded but the README is stale and still says future trees are included.
evidence: parseArgs backfill-graph-metadata.ts:71-96, isSpecFolder :99-101, walk :122-150, throw graph-metadata-parser.ts:1263-1265, EXCLUDED_DIRS :27, stale README scripts/graph/README.md:18
proposal: Add --spec-folder or a required positional target for non-dry-run backfills, reject unknown args, validate the resolved folder through supported-root and memory-scope checks. Keep broad mode behind --all. Update README and tests. This is the umbrella for a1-f1 and a6-f3.

title: Centralize canonical spec-folder identity and parent preservation
problem: description and graph metadata compute folder IDs differently, parent IDs are recomputed from the derived string, and mergeGraphMetadata spreads the refreshed payload without preserving existing parent_id.
evidence: generate-description.ts:77-84, folder-discovery.ts:884-895, graph strip graph-metadata-parser.ts:653-666, spec-doc-paths.ts:226-240, resolveParentId :669-679, mergeGraphMetadata :1145-1161
proposal: Introduce one resolveSpecFolderIdentity(absFolder) returning specFolder, parentId, childrenIds, specsRoot using supported roots only, always specs-root-relative. Use it in both generators. In refresh, preserve a non-null existing parent when recomputation returns null and emit a review flag. Umbrella for a6-f1 and a7-f1.

title: Make generated JSON enum-clean and idempotent
problem: Graph status is schema-permissive and normalizeDerivedStatus returns arbitrary text. Graph refresh is already idempotent, but description generation is not: it stamps lastUpdated and always writes, and aggregate cache stamps generated and folder lastUpdated with now.
evidence: graph-metadata-schema.ts:40-45, normalizeDerivedStatus graph-metadata-parser.ts:168-181, already idempotent graph :1181-1201 and :1275-1287 with test graph-metadata-refresh.vitest.ts:148-160, description not idempotent folder-discovery.ts:893-898 and :962-980 and :674-750
proposal: Add a closed DerivedStatus enum and idempotent write helpers for description.json and descriptions.json: compare canonical fields, preserve timestamps on no semantic delta, skip writes unless content changed. Keep canonical-save tracking allowed to bump lastUpdated. Umbrella for a3 and a4.

title: Refresh before indexing when source docs changed
problem: causal_summary derivation reads current spec.md, but memory_index_scan indexes existing metadata as found and parseMemoryContent trusts stored fields. Canonical saves refresh first, but manual or HVR edits bypass that path.
evidence: deriveCausalSummary graph-metadata-parser.ts:995-1007, scan memory-index.ts:537-543, parseMemoryContent memory-parser.ts:326-347, canonical refresh workflow.ts:1775-1780 and :1838-1844, incremental reindex indexing.ts:103-111
proposal: Add a graph_metadata_freshness guard comparing source_docs hashes against graph-metadata.json. When stale, a scoped generator run should refresh graph metadata first then call reindexSpecDocs(specFolder). Prefer content hash over mtime. Overlaps with a5-f4.
